lines.text: California.
I would have given almost anything that day to have had a movie
camera with a telephoto lens and from now on I will never be without one--
[unclear] another [unclear] story. When I landed at Yakima, Washing-
ton, airport I described what I had seen to my very good friend, [REDACTED]
who is the [REDACTED] He listened
patiently and was very courteous but in a joking way didn't believe me.
I did not [unclear] [unclear] [unclear] because there [unclear]
[unclear] I landed at Pendleton, Oregon that same day where I told a
number of pilot friends of mine what I had observed and they did not
scoff or laugh but suggested they might be guided missiles or something
new. In fact, several former Army pilots informed me that they had been
briefed before going into combat overseas that they might see objects of
similar shape and design as I described and assured me that I wasn't
dreaming or going crazy.
I quote [REDACTED] a former Army Air Force pilot who is now
operating dusting operations at Pendleton, Oregon "What you observed, I
am convinced, is some type of jet or rocket propelled ship that is in the
process of being tested by our government or even it could possibly be by
some foreign government".
Anyhow, the news that I had observed these spread very rapidly
[unclear]
parts of the world; and, to date I have not received one telephone call
or one letter of scoffing or disbelief. The only disbelief that I know
of was what was printed in the papers.
I look at this whole ordeal as not something funny as some people
have made it out to be. To me it is mighty serious and since I evidently
did observe something that at least Mr. John Doe on the street corner or
[unclear] Andrews on the [unclear] [unclear] [unclear] [unclear] [unclear] it
does not exist. Even though I openly invited an investigation by the Army
